channel.header: Discussions and reports on quality control testing and results

channel.name: interface_usability
channel.header: Discussions and feedbacks on the usability of vehicle interfaces
channel.purpose: To improve the usability of vehicle interfaces

post.message: Hello team, we are scheduling a quality control meeting for next Tuesday to discuss the recent test results. Please confirm your availability.

I've analyzed the latest customer satisfaction survey, and it seems users are generally happy with our interface's intuitiveness. However, there were some comments about the navigation system. I'll share the details in our next meeting.
We have just completed the quality inspection of the new battery packs. The results look promising, they are meeting all the performance indicators.
Good to hear, Kim. I'm also interested in the feedback on the navigation system. It will be useful for the upcoming interface design update.
Thanks for the update, Alex. I'll prepare the report on these results for the upper management.
Ethan, let's ensure we address any usability concerns from the feedback in the next design iteration. Meanwhile, I will also work on a detailed report about the customer satisfaction survey.
Hi team, I've just finished analyzing the customer complaint data from 2023-2024. The results are quite revealing.

The vast majority of complaints, around 68%, were related to our touchscreen interface, particularly accidental inputs and delayed response times. This is a significant pain point for our customers and it's leading to a higher control/display problem rate for our EVs.

This is a key area we should focus on for our 2026 EV models to reduce the problem rate and improve our PP100 score. The User Experience team may need to reconsider the balance between touchscreens and physical buttons/switches to address this issue. I'll share these findings with them as well.

Hi UX team, I just shared some insights with the Quality and Reliability team regarding customer complaints. 68% of them were about touchscreen interface issues in our EVs. We may need to prioritize physical buttons and switches in our design strategy for the 2026 models to address this.
